recommendation.summary: haxe.rust is viable for the current codexhx helper, credential-free headless, and selected fixture-backed adapter pressure slices, but it is not production-ready for broad Codex/Cafex replacement yet.
requiredBeforeBroadReplacement: Broaden haxe.rust parity evidence beyond selected helper/headless/adapter pressure fixtures.
Complete license/distribution review for haxe.rust GPL-3.0 with Codex/Cafex Apache-2.0 artifacts.
Reduce or explicitly native-own the 4 unsupported Cafex seam rows.
Run replacement rollback drills from the operator runbook.
Add broader live runtime parity evidence for model/provider, TUI, restart, and checkpoint ownership.

scorecard.rationale: Protocol IDs, JSON helpers, DTOs, enums, null scalar patterns, nullable interface values, try/catch returns, and reusable enum values now pass through haxe.rust after generic upstream fixes.
strengths: 10 of 10 pressure gaps are resolved upstream.
Protocol and JSON fixtures run through generated Cargo gates.
No raw Rust escape matches exist in current app/test Haxe source.
caveats: Current language proof is still fixture-backed and selected-slice scoped rather than broad upstream parity.

scorecard.rationale: Credential-free mock model, one-turn session, transcript/state, cancellation, and headless JSONL adapter slices are viable, but live model/TUI/restart ownership is outside current proof.
strengths: Headless runtime fixtures pass without model credentials.
Cancellation and transcript/state artifacts are deterministic.
Operator runbook keeps production default disabled.
caveats: Live model/provider traffic is not covered.
Live TUI, restart, and plan-checkpoint ownership remain unsupported Cafex rows.

scorecard.rationale: The project has kept interop typed and narrow: generated Rust is disposable, haxe.rust stays external, and app/test Haxe source has no raw Rust snippets.
strengths: Raw Rust escape pressure is 0 matches across 92 Haxe source/test files.
haxe.rust fixes are generic and made in ../haxe.rust.
Pin update workflow is scripted and gated.
caveats: Future metal/native boundaries still need real host-effect implementations.

scorecard.rationale: Process execution, sandbox policy, diagnostics redaction, and mutation controls fail closed in fixture gates, but real platform sandbox enforcement and production operational drills are not complete.
strengths: Process exec wrapper denies by default.
Sandbox gate fails closed for unsupported platforms.
Diagnostics redaction has fixture coverage.
caveats: Real OS sandbox enforcement remains a native boundary.
Security/licensing review is still required before distribution.

scorecard.rationale: Generated Rust builds and fixture checks are repeatable, and the profile policy aims for idiomatic output across portable/metal profiles, but no runtime performance benchmark suite exists yet.
strengths: Generated Cargo checks/tests run under locked gates.
Portable-first profile policy avoids premature raw-Rust rewrites.
Generated output is not committed as source.
caveats: No throughput/latency benchmark covers headless turns, tools, or adapters.
Clippy/fmt are advisory rather than required.
Metal-native performance claims need future focused benchmarks.

scorecard.rationale: The pressure-test process has produced upstreamable haxe.rust fixes, checked ledgers, and repeatable pins; broad replacement remains too costly while Cafex live runtime seams are unsupported.
strengths: 10 pressure gaps resolved upstream rather than carried as private compiler hacks.
Friction comparison supports selected adapter slices and review tooling.
Operator rollback and migration-mode policies are checked.
caveats: haxe.rust/Codex/Cafex drift must continue through pin and fixture gates.

scorecard.rationale: haxe.rust is GPL-3.0 while upstream Codex and Cafex pins are Apache-2.0; the experiment can continue locally, but release/bundling obligations are not reviewed.
strengths: haxe.rust remains an external checkout, not bundled.
Generated Rust is build output and not committed.
Operator runbook blocks production default changes before decision review.
caveats: Binary or bundled distribution needs license review.
Vendoring haxe.rust is deferred unless a later reproducibility decision and license review accept it.

feedsReplacementReview: Recommend HXCX-6.3 select helper-only, sidecar/headless, or selected adapter-slice mode.
Do not select broad replacement from current evidence.
Keep unsupported surfaces explicit and fail-closed.
Treat license/distribution as a release blocker, not a local-experiment blocker.
